title: Lebor Gabala Erenn: The Book of Invasions
source: Book of Leinster; Book of Ballymote
description: The mythological history of Ireland: five successive invasions from the creation of the world to the coming of the Gaels.
introduction: The Lebor Gabala Erenn (Book of Invasions) is Ireland's national creation myth — a pseudo-historical account of the successive peoples who settled the island, from the antediluvian Cessair through the Fomorians, the Fir Bolg, and the divine Tuatha De Danann, to the mortal Gaels. It is the framework within which all Irish mythology is organized.

translation.original_text: In principio fecit Deus celum et terram... Ériu, immorro, iar ndílinn, secht mbliadna fichet bái fás.
translation.text: In the beginning God made heaven and earth... Ireland, moreover, after the flood, was waste for twenty-seven years.

translation.original_text: Tuatha Dé Danann i n-insib tuaiscirt an domhain, oc foglaim druidechta ocus fessa ocus ammainsechta.
translation.text: The Tuatha De Danann were in the northern islands of the world, learning druidry and knowledge and prophecy.
